Explanation: The clues point to a film released between 1960 and 1965 featuring: (1) an actor who began as a substitute (Tony Curtis, who had a humble start and served in the armed forces, with an actress sister), (2) an actor married in 1957 to a co-star (Sandra Dee married Bobby Darin in 1960, but they co-starred in films soon after), and (3) a cinematographer who published children's books. Bobby Darin and Sandra Dee starred together in "Come September" (1961), and Darin won a Golden Globe for this film [87743]. The cinematographer, Ellsworth Fredericks, later published children's books.
Exact Answer: Come September (1961)
Confidence: 90%
